abort.no_devices_found: Can't find the system class needed for this component, make sure that your kernel is recent and the hardware is supported

under_voltage_not_supported.message: Under-voltage monitoring is not supported on this device.

under_voltage_detected.description:
Your device has reported a low-voltage condition. The Raspberry Pi monitors its power input and records an under-voltage event when the supply voltage drops below approximately 4.63V. This can lead to instability, unexpected reboots, poor performance, storage corruption, or unreliable operation of Home Assistant.

Common causes include:

- A power supply that cannot provide sufficient current.
- A low-quality or damaged USB power cable causing voltage drop.
- High-demand USB devices connected to the system.
- Loose power connections.

To resolve this issue:

- Use the official Raspberry Pi power supply or a high-quality equivalent.
- Replace the power cable with a shorter, higher-quality cable.
- Disconnect unnecessary USB devices and check whether the warning stops.

After correcting the power issue, monitor the system to ensure no additional under-voltage events are reported.
under_voltage_detected.title: Under-voltage detected
